subject: Alaska.Ai — LinkedIn Carousel No. 9 — 2026-07-17 — No Road Out. Quinhagak Flies Its Own Eyes
html_body:
Alaska.Ai · LinkedIn Carousel No. 9

2026-07-17 · 9 slides · branch tsturg/ecstatic-cerf-zo159m

1 · Upload the document

Download carousel.pdf (7.86 MB, vector text) → LinkedIn → start a post → add a document → upload the PDF → title it: No Road Out. Quinhagak Flies Its Own Eyes

2 · Paste the post copy

Quinhagak has no road out, so the roadless Yup'ik village learned to fly its own drones.

In the Yukon-Kuskokwim Delta, search and rescue calls come weekly, sometimes daily, and outside operators cannot read the airspace. So the village corporation trains its own FAA Part 107 pilots and runs its own machine vision, a neural network that spots a small aluminum boat on a search grid.

The deck walks through the weekly calls, the class of new pilots, the machine the community actually owns, and the honest split between real AI and the human eyes reading thermal and archaeology pictures.

When your community's land, water, and people are watched from the air, who should own the machine doing the watching?

#ruralAlaska #YukonKuskokwimDelta #droneSAR

3 · Post, then paste this as the FIRST comment (within a minute)

Sources for this one, primary first.
KYUK, Quinhagak locals bridge drones and traditional knowledge, now they are teaching others, July 14 2026, https://www.kyuk.org/science-and-environment/2026-07-14/quinhagak-locals-bridge-drones-and-traditional-knowledge-now-theyre-teaching-others-how-to-do-it
Alaska Public Media, Alaska News Nightly, western Alaska drone program takes flight, July 14 2026, https://alaskapublic.org/programs/alaska-news-nightly/2026-07-14/alaska-news-nightly-tuesday-july-14-2026
Nalaquq, the Alaska Native-owned subsidiary of Qanirtuuq, https://nalaquq.com/about-us/
JCMC, the peer-reviewed paper behind the search-and-rescue vessel-detection CNN and the salmon-counting pipeline, https://academic.oup.com/jcmc/article/28/5/zmad030/7248802
Every Alaska AI decision we track, with live deadlines, lives at alaskaaihq.com
If you know Quinhagak or Nalaquq firsthand, reply and add what the reporting missed.

Automation changes this run (1)

The routine modified its own machinery this run. If a later week looks worse, this dated section is the rollback trail (each set reverts as one commit).
KindChangeRollback
fix
scripts		New scripts/copy_sync_check.py plus routine wiring (Phase 8 post-hand-edit pre-flight + Phase 11 completion-gate guard): for every slide string in copy.json['slides'] it verifies the string is present in that slide's RENDERED text (render_report.json text_nodes[].text). One-directional (authored copy must not go stale vs the render); alphanumeric-only, case/whitespace/punctuation-insensitive matching; long strings matched on their leading 40 alnum chars to tolerate render.py's .slice(0,80) node-text truncation, short display strings (the record-sync risk class) in full. Reads only; never edits copy.json or slides.
why: Run 2026-07-17 record-sync gap: the showrunner hand-edited slide-05's kicker in the HTML ('HOW IT STARTED' -> 'BEFORE THE CLASS') after a flow-critic note, but copy.json we		revert the upgrade(2026-07-17) commit (deletes scripts/copy_sync_check.py and the two Phase 8 / Phase 11 prompt lines; the pipeline reverts to no record-sync check)
ca63631

Aftercare (this is where reach is won)

  • Post Tuesday to Thursday, 8 to 11am Alaska time (day signal is the robust one).
  • Paste the first-comment sources block within about 60 seconds of posting.
  • Reply substantively to every comment in the first 60 to 90 minutes (golden hour drives up to 2.4x reach).
  • Upload the PDF as a DOCUMENT and set the title exactly; do not add a link in the post body.
  • Consider cross-posting from a personal profile (personal carousels earn markedly more reach than page posts).
  • Optional: type a short alt text per slide in LinkedIn's upload UI (documents carry no alt text otherwise).
